name: Pyrex Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Pyrex. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Pyrex surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good absorption in surface contaminants while minimizing bulk Pyrex absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning with adequate energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage and thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Energy density threshold for effective contaminant removal without damaging Pyrex substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with controlled thermal effects

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap percentage for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of delicate glasses like Pyrex to avoid thermal cracking; starting at 100W for light contamination, max below damage threshold (~500W for short pulses). Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics and Coherent for 1064 nm systems; academic paper on glass cleaning (Opt. Lasers Eng., 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for 1064 nm cleaning of Pyrex; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (up to 3000 mm/s) for light; 500 mm/s optimal for balance of efficiency and safety. Source: Industry standards from Laser Cleaning Europe guidelines and equipment manuals (e.g., Adapt Laser).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for Pyrex to ablate contaminants without deep penetration; 20 ns minimizes heat-affected zone (HAZ) due to Pyrex's low thermal conductivity (~1 W/mK); picosecond (ps) possible but less common industrially. Source: Laser physics research (Appl. Phys. A, 2015) on glass ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064 nm cleaning to ensure pulse overlap without excessive heat accumulation on Pyrex (melting point ~820°C); 50 kHz balances speed and thermal management; higher rates increase efficiency but risk stress. Source: Academic literature (J. Appl. Phys., 2020) on pulsed laser cleaning of borosilicates.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal on Pyrex; below damage threshold (~5-10 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate absorption); 1 J/cm² recommended for safe cleaning without substrate ablation. Source: Ablation threshold studies (Opt. Express, 2017) for borosilicate glass.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise cleaning of Pyrex surfaces; smaller (100-300 μm) for fine details, larger for area coverage; 300 μm typical to control energy density on reflective/low-conductivity Pyrex. Source: Equipment specifications from Trumpf and SPI Lasers.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-3) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on Pyrex without cumulative thermal damage. Source: Industry case studies (Proc. SPIE, 2019) on glass surface preparation.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage in scan patterns; 50% optimal for Pyrex to avoid gaps or overexposure, considering beam Gaussian profile. Source: Process optimization studies (Surf. Coat. Technol., 2021) for laser cleaning applications.
